sections.name: Omelia
sections.text: *Nethys Note: No description has been provided for this NPC.*

traits.name: Unique
traits.text: A rules element with this trait is one-of-a-kind. The DC of Recall Knowledge checks related to creatures with this trait is increased by 10.

traits.name: Medium
traits.text: *Space:* 5 feet, *Reach (Tall):* 5 feet, *Reach (Long):* 5 feet

traits.name: Ghoul
traits.text: Ghouls are vile undead creatures that feast on flesh.

traits.name: Undead
traits.text: Once living, these creatures were infused after death with void energy and soul-corrupting unholy magic. When reduced to 0 Hit Points, an undead creature is destroyed. Undead creatures are damaged by vitality energy and are healed by void energy, and don’t benefit from healing vitality effects.

traits.name: Unholy
traits.text: Effects with the unholy trait are tied to powerful magical forces of cruelty and sin. They often have stronger effects on holy creatures. Creatures with this trait are strongly devoted to unholy causes, and often have weakness to holy. If a creature with weakness to unholy uses an unholy item or effect, it takes damage from its weakness.

family.name: Ghoul

sections.name: Creating Ghouls
sections.text: The monsters in this section are built from the ground up. Custom-building ghouls is recommended if you have time, using the special abilities listed here but creating the other statistics to match what you want the ghoul to be. You can also turn a living creature into a ghoul by completing the following steps. First, increase the creature's level by 1 and change its statistics as follows.

changes.text: - Increase its AC, attack bonuses, DCs, saving throws, Perception, and skill modifiers by 1.

- Increase its damage with Strikes and other offensive abilities by 1. If the creature is limited in how often it can use an ability (such as dragon breath), increase that ability's damage by 2 instead.

subtypes.name: Ghoul Abilities
subtypes.text:
A ghoul gains the undead and ghoul traits, and it usually becomes unholy. If the base creature has any abilities that come from it being a living creature, it loses them. It also loses any traits that represent its life as a living creature, such as human and humanoid. You might also need to adjust abilities that conflict with the theme of a ghoul. All ghouls gain the following abilities. The save DC for all abilities uses the high spell DC of the ghoul's level.  
  
Darkvision  
Void Healing  
 Immunities bleed, death effects, disease, paralyzed, poison, unconscious  
Stench (aura, olfactory) 10 feet. Use the moderate spell DC for the ghoul's level.

abilities.name: Burrow
abilities.text: The ghoul gains a burrow speed of 5 feet.

abilities.name: Claws
abilities.text: If the creature had hands, it gains a claw Strike (an agile unarmed attack that deals slashing damage plus Grab). If it had any agile attacks, the damage dealt by its claws should be roughly the same as the damage dealt by those attacks. If it had only non-agile attacks, its claws should deal three-quarters that damage.

abilities.name: Jaws
abilities.text: If the creature had a mouth, it gains a jaws Strike (an unarmed attack that deals slashing damage). The damage amount should be the same as the creature's non-agile attacks.

abilities.name: Consume Flesh
abilities.text: [one-action]  (manipulate);

abilities.name: Requirements
abilities.text: The ghoul is adjacent to the corpse of a creature that died within the last hour;

abilities.name: Effect
abilities.text: The ghoul devours a chunk of the corpse and regains 1d6 Hit Points plus 1d6 for every 2 levels the ghoul has. It can regain Hit Points from any given corpse only once.

abilities.name: Ghoul Whispers
abilities.text: [one-action]  (auditory, linguistic, occult);

abilities.name: Requirement
abilities.text: A grabbed, paralyzed, restrained, or unconscious creature is within the ghoul's reach;

abilities.name: Effect
abilities.text: The ghoul whispers dark thoughts and vile cravings into the creature's ears. The creature must save against the forbidden cravings curse.

abilities.name: Forbidden Cravings
abilities.text: (curse) A creature can still eat and drink while sickened by this curse;

abilities.name: Saving Throw
abilities.text: Will, using the high spell DC for the ghoul's level;

abilities.name: Stage 1
abilities.text: carrier with no ill effects (1 day);

abilities.name: Stage 2
abilities.text: 2d6 void damage and the target is sickened 1 until it consumes raw meat (1 day);

abilities.name: Stage 3
abilities.text: as stage 2;

abilities.name: Stage 4
abilities.text: as stage 2 unless the target has consumed raw meat in the past 24 hours, then it takes 4d6 void damage and is sickened 2 until it consumes raw meat;

abilities.name: Stage 5
abilities.text: if the creature has eaten raw meat in the past 24 hours, it dies and rises as a ghoul, if not, it returns to stage 4

abilities.name: Grave Knowledge
abilities.text: (occult)

abilities.name: Frequency
abilities.text: once per hour;

abilities.name: Effect
abilities.text: The ghoul calls upon knowledge it retains from one creature it has consumed in the past 7 days. The ghoul attempts a skill check using a skill in which the consumed creature was trained (if it's unclear whether the creature was trained, the GM decides). The ghoul is treated  as trained and uses the high skill modifier for the ghoul's level. This takes the same amount of actions or time as usual for the check.

abilities.name: Swift Leap
abilities.text: [one-action]  (move) The ghoul jumps up to half its Speed. This movement doesn't trigger reactions.

family.text: Few creatures are more ubiquitous to sinister locations such as lonely graveyards and ruined crypts than the flesh-eating undead known as ghouls.

universal_monster_ability.name: Void Healing
universal_monster_ability.text: A creature with void healing draws health from void energy rather than vitality energy. It is damaged by vitality damage and is not healed by healing vitality effects. It does not take void damage, and it is healed by void effects that heal undead.

traits.name: Aura
traits.text: An aura is an emanation that continually ebbs out from you, affecting creatures within a certain radius. Aura can also refer to the magical signature of an item.

traits.name: Olfactory
traits.text: An olfactory effect can affect only creatures that can smell it. This applies only to olfactory parts of the effect, as determined by the GM.

universal_monster_ability.name: Stench
universal_monster_ability.text: A creature entering the aura or starting its turn in the area must succeed at a Fortitude save or become sickened 1 (plus slowed 1 as long as it's sickened on a critical failure). A creature that succeeds at its save or recovers from being sickened is temporarily immune to all stench auras for 1 minute.

traits.name: Aura
traits.text: An aura is an emanation that continually ebbs out from you, affecting creatures within a certain radius. Aura can also refer to the magical signature of an item.

traits.name: Olfactory
traits.text: An olfactory effect can affect only creatures that can smell it. This applies only to olfactory parts of the effect, as determined by the GM.

traits.name: Finesse
traits.text: You can use your Dexterity modifier instead of your Strength modifier on attack rolls using this melee weapon. You still calculate damage using Strength.

traits.name: Finesse
traits.text: You can use your Dexterity modifier instead of your Strength modifier on attack rolls using this melee weapon. You still calculate damage using Strength.

traits.name: Deadly
traits.text: On a critical hit, the weapon adds a weapon damage die of the listed size. Roll this after doubling the weapon’s damage. This increases to two dice if the weapon has a _greater striking rune_ and three dice if the weapon has a _major striking rune_. For instance, a rapier with a _greater striking rune_ deals 2d8 extra piercing damage on a critical hit. An ability that changes the size of the weapon’s normal damage dice doesn’t change the size of its deadly die.

traits.name: Magical
traits.text: Something with the magical trait is imbued with magical energies not tied to a specific tradition of magic. Some items or effects are closely tied to a particular tradition of magic. In these cases, the item has the arcane, divine, occult, or primal trait instead of the magical trait. Any of these traits indicate that the item is magical.

traits.name: Propulsive
traits.text: You add half your Strength modifier (if positive) to damage rolls with a propulsive ranged weapon. If you have a negative Strength modifier, you add your full Strength modifier instead.

traits.name: Range
traits.text: These attacks will either list a finite range or a range increment, which follows the normal rules for range increments.

traits.name: Reload
traits.text:
While all weapons need some amount of time to get into position, many ranged weapons also need to be loaded and reloaded. This entry indicates how many Interact actions it takes to reload such weapons. This can be 0 if drawing ammunition and firing the weapon are part of the same action. If an item takes 2 or more actions to reload, the GM determines whether they must be performed together as an activity, or you can spend some of those actions during one turn and the rest during your next turn.  
  
An item with an entry of “—” must be drawn to be thrown, which usually takes an Interact action just like drawing any other weapon. Reloading a ranged weapon and drawing a thrown weapon both require a free hand. Switching your grip to free a hand and then to place your hands in the grip necessary to wield the weapon are both included in the actions you spend to reload a weapon.

traits.name: Volley
traits.text: This ranged weapon is less effective at close distances. Your attacks against targets that are at a distance within the range listed take a –2 penalty.

traits.name: Manipulate
traits.text: You must physically manipulate an item or make gestures to use an action with this trait. Creatures without a suitable appendage can’t perform actions with this trait. Manipulate actions often trigger reactions.

traits.name: Auditory
traits.text: Auditory actions and effects rely on sound. An action with the auditory trait can be successfully performed only if the creature using the action can speak or otherwise produce the required sounds. A spell or effect with the auditory trait has its effect only if the target can hear it. This applies only to sound-based parts of the effect, as determined by the GM. This is different from a sonic effect, which still affects targets who can’t hear it (such as deaf targets) as long as the effect itself makes sound.

traits.name: Linguistic
traits.text: An effect with this trait depends on language comprehension. A linguistic effect that targets a creature works only if the target understands the language you are using.

traits.name: Occult
traits.text:
This magic comes from the occult tradition, calling upon bizarre and ephemeral mysteries. Anything with this trait is magical.  
  
A creature with this trait is primarily constituted of or has a strong connection to occult magic.

traits.name: Occult
traits.text:
This magic comes from the occult tradition, calling upon bizarre and ephemeral mysteries. Anything with this trait is magical.  
  
A creature with this trait is primarily constituted of or has a strong connection to occult magic.

ability.name: Hunt Prey

traits.name: Concentrate
traits.text: An action with this trait requires a degree of mental concentration and discipline.

ability.text: Omelia designates a single creature she can see and hear, or one she's Tracking, as her prey. She gains a +2 circumstance bonus to Perception checks to Seek the prey and to Survival checks to Track the prey. The first time Omelia hits her designated prey in a round, she deals an additional 1d8 precision damage. These effects last until she uses Hunt Prey again.

traits.name: Move
traits.text: An action with this trait involves moving from one space to another.
